state.output:
<path>/Users/ubuntu/1-project/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # trae-plugin-cc
2: 
3: A **dual-platform** plugin that bridges [**Claude Code**](https://claude.ai/code) and [**OpenCode**](https://opencode.ai) with ByteDance [**Trae Agent**](https://docs.trae.cn/cli) (`trae-cli`). Delegate natural language tasks, run code reviews, manage sessions, and enable Agent-to-Agent communication — all inheriting trae-cli's OAuth2 auth (no extra API keys needed).
4: 
5: ## Features
6: 
7: | Feature | Description |
8: |---------|-------------|
9: | **Task Delegation** | Send natural language prompts to Trae Agent with YOLO mode, session resume, git worktree isolation, and fine-grained tool permissions |
10: | **Code Review** | Automatic git diff + professional review — standard or adversarial (extremely strict), with smart base branch detection and change-size estimation |
11: | **Session Management** | Query trae-cli's history: conversations, tool calls, context summaries, topic search, and bulk cleanup |
12: | **ACP Protocol** | JSON-RPC over STDIO for inter-agent communication (start/stop servers, discover agents, execute/stream tasks) |
13: | **Background Tasks** | Long-running tasks with PID tracking, status listing, result retrieval, and cancellation |
14: | **Lifecycle Hooks** | Auto-triggered on session start/end/stop: env checks, background task reminders, uncommitted-change gates |
15: 
16: ## Architecture
17: 
18: ```
19: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
20: │                        trae-plugin-cc                             │
21: │                                                                    │
22: │  ┌──────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────────────┐   │
23: │  │    Claude Code       │          │       OpenCode           │   │
24: │  ├──────────────────────┤          ├──────────────────────────┤   │
25: │  │ 10 Slash Commands    │          │ 9  Bun Tools (.ts)       │   │
26: │  │ 4  MCP Tools (.json) │          │ 10 Command Docs (.md)    │   │
27: │  │ 4  Lifecycle Hooks   │          │ Event Hooks (.ts)        │   │
28: │  └──────────┬───────────┘          └──────────┬───────────────┘   │
29: │             │                                  │                    │
30: │  ┌──────────▼──────────────────────────────────▼───────────────┐   │
31: │  │              Core CLI (src/ → dist/index.js)                 │   │
32: │  │  setup | run | review | adversarial-review | sessions |      │   │
33: │  │  acp | status | result | cancel | rescue | hooks            │   │
34: │  └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘   │
35: │                             │                                       │
36: │                    ┌─────────▼──────────┐                          │
37: │                    │    trae-cli        │                          │
38: │                    │  (OAuth2 inherit)  │                          │
39: │                    └────────────────────┘                          │
40: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
41: ```
42: 
43: Both platforms share the same core CLI (`dist/index.js`). Claude Code invokes via slash commands/MCP/hooks; OpenCode invokes via Bun tools that `spawn("node", [dist/index.js, ...])`.
44: 
45: ## Prerequisites
46: 
47: - **Node.js 18+** (runtime)
48: - **trae-cli** installed and authenticated
49: 
50: ```bash
51: # Verify trae-cli
52: trae-cli --help
53: 
54: # Verify auth (should show model + login URL)
55: cat ~/.trae/trae_cli.yaml
56: ```
57: 
58: ## Installation
59: 
60: ### Claude Code
61: 
62: **Option 1: Marketplace (recommended)**
63: 
64: ```bash
65: claude plugin marketplace add https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc
66: claude plugin install trae
67: claude plugin list | grep trae
68: ```
69: 
70: Scope: `--scope user` (global) | `--scope project` (current project) | `--scope local` (current session)
71: 
72: **Option 2: Local directory**
73: 
74: ```bash
75: git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc.git
76: cd trae-plugin-cc && npm install && npm run build
77: claude plugin marketplace add "$(pwd)"
78: claude plugin install trae
79: ```
80: 
81: ### OpenCode
82: 
83: ```bash
84: git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc.git
85: cd trae-plugin-cc && npm install && npm run build
86: cd .opencode && npm install && cd ..
87: ```
88: 
89: OpenCode auto-discovers tools in `.opencode/tools/` and commands in `.opencode/commands/`.
90: 
91: ### Verify Installation
92: 
93: ```bash
94: node dist/index.js setup
95: ```
96: 
97: Expected output:
98: ```
99: ✅ trae-cli 已安装并可用！
100: 
101: ## 认证状态
102:   已认证: ✅
103:   模型: GLM-5
104:   登录地址: https://console.enterprise.trae.cn
105: ```
106: 
107: ## Quick Start
108: 
109: ### Claude Code (slash commands)
110: 
111: ```
112: /trae:run "重构用户认证模块"           # execute task
113: /trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo         # skip tool confirmations
114: /trae:review                           # standard review
115: /trae:adversarial-review               # adversarial review
116: /trae:sessions list                    # list sessions
117: /trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
118: ```
119: 
120: ### OpenCode (Bun tools)
121: 
122: OpenCode Agent calls tools directly: `trae-run`, `trae-review`, `trae-sessions`, `trae-acp`, etc. See [Commands Reference](#commands-reference) for full parameter details.
123: 
124: ## Commands Reference
125: 
126: All CLI commands: `node dist/index.js <command> [args]`
127: 
128: ### `setup`
129: 
130: Verify trae-cli installation and auth status.
131: 
132: ```bash
133: /trae:setup          # Claude Code
134: # OpenCode: trae-setup tool (no args)
135: ```
136: 
137: ---
138: 
139: ### `run "prompt"`
140: 
141: Delegate a natural language task to Trae Agent.
142: 
143: ```bash
144: /trae:run "描述任务" [options]
145: ```
146: 
147: | Option | Alias | Description |
148: |--------|-------|-------------|
149: | `--background` | | Run in background (returns job ID) |
150: | `--json` | | Structured JSON output with `session_id` |
151: | `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool permission confirmations |
152: | `--resume [ID]` | | Resume session (omit ID = auto-resume latest) |
153: | `--session-id <id>` | | Specify new session ID |
154: | `--worktree [name]` | `-w` | Isolated git worktree (omit name = `__auto__`) |
155: | `--allowed-tool <n>` | | Auto-approve tool (repeatable) |
156: | `--disallowed-tool <n>` | | Auto-reject tool (repeatable) |
157: | `--query-timeout <d>` | | Per-query timeout (e.g. `30s`, `5m`) |
158: | `--bash-tool-timeout <d>` | | Bash tool timeout |
159: | `-c <k=v>` | | Config override (repeatable) |
160: | `--inject-context <id>` | | Inject another session's context |
161: 
162: **Examples:**
163: ```bash
164: /trae:run "重构认证模块" --background --json
165: /trae:run "继续" --resume --yolo
166: /trae:run "修复 bug" --worktree --allowed-tool Edit --allowed-tool Bash
167: ```
168: 
169: ---
170: 
171: ### `review` / `adversarial-review`
172: 
173: Code review with automatic git diff.
174: 
175: ```bash
176: /trae:review [options]                   # standard
177: /trae:adversarial-review [options]       # adversarial (strict)
178: # OpenCode: trae-review tool with adversarial: true/false
179: ```
180: 
181: | Option | Alias | Description |
182: |--------|-------|-------------|
183: | `--base <branch>` | | Base branch (auto-detected: main/master/develop) |
184: | `--background` | | Run in background (recommended for large diffs) |
185: | `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool confirmations |
186: | `--json` | | Structured JSON output |
187: | `--session-id <id>` | | Specify session ID |
188: 
189: Auto-detects base branch and estimates review size, recommending background mode for large changes.
190: 
191: ---
192: 
193: ### `sessions <action>`
194: 
195: Manage trae-cli's historical sessions (reads from file cache directly — no HTTP calls).
196: 
197: ```bash
198: /trae:sessions <action> [options]
199: ```
200: 
201: | Action | Args | Options | Description |
202: |--------|------|---------|-------------|
203: | `list` | | `--cwd`, `--limit` (default 20) | List all sessions |
204: | `recent` | | `--cwd` | Most recent session for cwd |
205: | `detail <id>` | `<session-id>` | | Session metadata + event counts |
206: | `conversation <id>` | `<session-id>` | `--limit` (default 50) | Conversation messages |
207: | `tools <id>` | `<session-id>` | | Tool call stats + records (up to 30) |
208: | `context <id>` | `<session-id>` | | Full context summary |
209: | `find <topic>` | `<topic>` | | Search by keyword |
210: | `delete <id>` | `<session-id>` | | Delete a session |
211: | `delete-smoke` | | | Bulk delete sessions with "smoke" in ID/title |
212: 
213: ---
214: 
215: ### `acp <action>`
216: 
217: ACP protocol: **JSON-RPC over STDIO** (not HTTP). Enables Agent-to-Agent communication.
218: 
219: ```bash
220: /trae:acp <action> [options]
221: ```
222: 
223: | Action | Args | Description |
224: |--------|------|-------------|
225: | `start` | `--yolo`, `--allowed-tool`, `--disabled-tool` | Start ACP Server (blocks, keeps STDIO pipe open) |
226: | `stop` | | Stop ACP Server |
227: | `status` | | Check server status |
228: | `agents` | | Discover available agents (auto-starts server if needed) |
229: | `run <prompt>` | `<prompt>` | Execute task via ACP (auto-starts server) |
230: | `stream <prompt>` | `<prompt>` | Stream task output in real-time (auto-starts server) |
231: 
232: > **Note**: `acp start` is a blocking call — it holds the process alive for the STDIO pipe. Use `agents`, `run`, or `stream` for interactive use (they auto-start the server).
233: 
234: ---
235: 
236: ### `status`
237: 
238: List all background tasks (reads `.claude-trae-plugin/` for PID files).
239: 
240: ```bash
241: /trae:status
242: ```
243: 
244: ---
245: 
246: ### `result <task-id>`
247: 
248: Get output of a background task.
249: 
250: ```bash
251: /trae:result <task-id>
252: ```
253: 
254: ---
255: 
256: ### `cancel <task-id>`
257: 
258: Force-kill a background task (SIGKILL).
259: 
260: ```bash
261: /trae:cancel <task-id>
262: ```
263: 
264: ---
265: 
266: ### `rescue`
267: 
268: Diagnose recent task failures, collect error logs and git status.
269: 
270: ```bash
271: /trae:rescue [--context text]
272: ```
273: 
274: | Option | Description |
275: |--------|-------------|
276: | `--context <text>` | Additional context for diagnosis |
277: 
278: ---
279: 
280: ### Lifecycle Hooks
281: 
282: Auto-triggered — no manual invocation needed.
283: 
284: | Hook | Trigger | Behavior |
285: |------|---------|----------|
286: | `SessionStart` | Claude Code startup / OpenCode session.created | Check trae-cli, remind background tasks |
287: | `SessionEnd` | Claude Code exit / OpenCode session.deleted | Clean old logs, remind running tasks |
288: | `Stop` | User stops operation | Gate on uncommitted changes |
289: | `PostToolUse` | After `/trae:review` | Log review results |
290: 
291: ---
292: 
293: ### `hooks <type>` (internal)
294: 
295: Internal hook handler — invoked by lifecycle scripts, not users.
296: 
297: ```bash
298: trae-plugin-cc hooks session-start|session-end|stop-gate
299: ```
300: 
301: ## Project Structure
302: 
303: ```
304: trae-plugin-cc/
305: ├── .claude-plugin/          # Claude Code plugin + marketplace manifest
306: ├── commands/                # Slash command docs (*.md)
307: ├── .mcp.json                # 4 MCP tool definitions
308: ├── hooks/hooks.json         # Claude Code lifecycle hooks
309: ├── scripts/                 # Hook scripts (*.mjs)
310: ├── src/                     # Core CLI (TypeScript → dist/index.js)
311: │   ├── index.ts             # Entrypoint
312: │   ├── commands/            # Command handlers
313: │   └── utils/               # TraeExecutor, SessionReader, AuthBridge, AcpClient...
314: ├── .opencode/               # OpenCode integration
315: │   ├── tools/               # 9 Bun tools (*.ts)
316: │   ├── commands/            # 10 command docs (*.md)
317: │   ├── plugins/             # Event hooks
318: │   └── package.json         # OpenCode deps (@opencode-ai/plugin)
319: └── tests/                   # Jest tests
320: ```
321: 
322: ## Development
323: 
324: ```bash
325: npm run build          # esbuild → dist/index.js (run after ANY src/ change)
326: npm test               # jest (all tests)
327: cd .opencode && npm install && cd ..   # OpenCode deps (once)
328: ```
329: 
330: ### ⚠️ Build Rule
331: 
332: `npm run build` uses **esbuild** (bundled single file with shebang) — this is the **production entrypoint**. `npm run build:tsc` produces separate `.js` files for module imports but is not used at runtime.
333: 
334: | Command | Output | Used By |
335: |---------|--------|---------|
336: | `npm run build` | `dist/index.js` (bundled) | **Runtime CLI** |
337: | `npm run build:tsc` | `dist/**/*.js` (separate) | Direct module imports |
338: 
339: **Always run `npm run build` after modifying `src/` before testing.** This applies to OpenCode too — its tools spawn `dist/index.js`.
340: 
341: ## Session Cache Paths
342: 
343: trae-cli stores sessions on disk (read directly by this plugin):
344: 
345: | Platform | Path |
346: |----------|------|
347: | macOS | `~/Library/Caches/trae_cli/sessions/` |
348: | Linux | `~/.cache/trae_cli/sessions/` |
349: 
350: ## License
351: 
352: MIT

(End of file - total 352 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# trae-plugin-cc

A **dual-platform** plugin that bridges [**Claude Code**](https://claude.ai/code) and [**OpenCode**](https://opencode.ai) with ByteDance [**Trae Agent**](https://docs.trae.cn/cli) (`trae-cli`). Delegate natural language tasks, run code reviews, manage sessions, and enable Agent-to-Agent communication — all inheriting trae-cli's OAuth2 auth (no extra API keys needed).

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Task Delegation** | Send natural language prompts to Trae Agent with YOLO mode, session resume, git worktree isolation, and fine-grained tool permissions |
| **Code Review** | Automatic git diff + professional review — standard or adversarial (extremely strict), with smart base branch detection and change-size estimation |
| **Session Management** | Query trae-cli's history: conversations, tool calls, context summaries, topic search, and bulk cleanup |
| **ACP Protocol** | JSON-RPC over STDIO for inter-agent communication (start/stop servers, discover agents, execute/stream tasks) |
| **Background Tasks** | Long-running tasks with PID tracking, status listing, result retrieval, and cancellation |
| **Lifecycle Hooks** | Auto-triggered on session start/end/stop: env checks, background task reminders, uncommitted-change gates |

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        trae-plugin-cc                             │

input.newString:
## Quick Start

Both Claude Code and OpenCode support the same `/trae:*` command interface:

```
/trae:run "重构用户认证模块"           # execute task
/trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo         # skip tool confirmations
/trae:review                           # standard review
/trae:adversarial-review               # adversarial review
/trae:sessions list                    # list sessions
/trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
```

> **Note**: Claude Code additionally registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation beyond what the user can trigger manually.
input.oldString:
## Quick Start

### Claude Code (slash commands)

```
/trae:run "重构用户认证模块"           # execute task
/trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo         # skip tool confirmations
/trae:review                           # standard review
/trae:adversarial-review               # adversarial review
/trae:sessions list                    # list sessions
/trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
```

### OpenCode (Bun tools)

OpenCode Agent calls tools directly: `trae-run`, `trae-review`, `trae-sessions`, `trae-acp`, etc. See [Commands Reference](#commands-reference) for full parameter details.

state.output:
task_id: ses_24acb5751ffefUnJHTwKdEQGjn (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Here is my detailed analysis:

---

## Detailed Analysis: How OpenCode vs Claude Code Invokes Trae Commands

### 1. OpenCode Command Definition Files (.opencode/commands/*.md)

**ALL 10 command files use the SAME format:**
- **Frontmatter:** Only a `description:` field (no `/trae:` prefix anywhere in frontmatter)
- **Execution instructions:** `node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" <raw-command> $ARGUMENTS`

For example, `trae-run.md`:
```
description: Delegates a natural-language task to Trae Agent...
---
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" run $ARGUMENTS
```

Full list of raw command names used (no prefix):
- `run`
- `review`
- `adversarial-review`
- `sessions`
- `acp`
- `setup`
- `status`
- `result`
- `cancel`
- `rescue`

**Zero instances of `/trae:` appear in any `.opencode/commands/` file.**

---

### 2. OpenCode Tool Files (.opencode/tools/*.ts)

**ALL 9 tools use the exact same invocation pattern:**
```typescript
const DIST_INDEX = path.join(PLUGIN_DIR, "dist", "index.js")
...
const cliArgs: string[] = ["node", DIST_INDEX, "<raw-command>"]
```

Every single tool, confirmed from source:

| File | Line | Spawn Command |
|------|------|--------------|
| `trae-run.ts` | 44 | `["node", DIST_INDEX, "run"]` |
| `trae-review.ts` | 26 | `["node", DIST_INDEX, command]` (either `"review"` or `"adversarial-review"`) |
| `trae-sessions.ts` | 40 | `["node", DIST_INDEX, "sessions", args.action]` |
| `trae-acp.ts` | 27 | `["node", DIST_INDEX, "acp", args.action]` |
| `trae-setup.ts` | 13 | `$`node ${DIST_INDEX} setup`` |
| `trae-status.ts` | 13 | `$`node ${DIST_INDEX} status`` |
| `trae-result.ts` | 15 | `$`node ${DIST_INDEX} result ${args.task_id}`` |
| `trae-cancel.ts` | 15 | `$`node ${DIST_INDEX} cancel ${args.task_id}`` |
| `trae-rescue.ts` | 18 | `["node", DIST_INDEX, "rescue"]` |

**NONE of them include `/trae:` prefix.**

**The ONLY places `/trae:` appears in OpenCode tools** are in the *user-facing description strings* of two files:
- `trae-cancel.ts` line 9: `"通过 \`/trae:status\` 或 status 工具获取任务 ID"` -- This is just a user hint telling the agent how to find task IDs.
- `trae-result.ts` line 9: `"通过 \`/trae:status\` 或 status 工具获取任务 ID"` -- Same reference.

These are NOT invocation code. They are just describing to the agent that a user might use `/trae:status` in Claude Code to find task IDs.

---

### 3. OpenCode Plugins (.opencode/plugins/)

The trae hooks file (`trae-hooks.ts`) spawns lifecycle hook scripts, not CLI commands. Pattern:
```typescript
return ["node", path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, script), hookType]
```
No `/trae:` prefix anywhere.

---

### 4. Claude Code Command Definition Files (commands/*.md)

**ALL 10 command files use `/trae:name` format:**

- `run.md` heading: `# /trae:run` -- all examples: `/trae:run "任务"`
- `review.md` heading: `# /trae:review` -- all examples: `/trae:review`
- `setup.md` heading: `# /trae:setup`
- `sessions.md` heading: `# /trae:sessions` -- all examples: `/trae:sessions list`
- etc.

**BUT** the internal execution line at the bottom still uses the raw command:
```
**Internal Execution:**
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" run "任务描述" [options]
```

So even in Claude Code, `/trae:run` is just the **slash command name** that the user types. Claude Code maps `trae:run` to running `node dist/index.js run` (the raw command).

---

### 5. The src/index.ts CLI Router

The CLI in `src/index.ts` (`dist/index.js`) parses command as a positional argument:
```
trae-plugin-cc <command>
  setup, run, review, adversarial-review, sessions, acp, status, result, cancel, rescue, hooks
```

It expects the raw command name (e.g., `run`, `sessions`), NOT the `/trae:` format. The `/trae:` prefix is stripped/translated before reaching the CLI.

---

### Summary

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| Does OpenCode use `/trae:run` format? | **NO.** OpenCode never uses `/trae:` prefix in any invocation. |
| What format does OpenCode use? | Raw command names: `node dist/index.js run`, `node dist/index.js sessions list`, etc. |
| How do OpenCode tools spawn commands? | Via `bun`'s `$` template: `$`node ${DIST_INDEX} <command> ${args}`` |
| What does `.opencode/commands/*.md` frontmatter look like? | Just `description: <text>` -- no command name, no `/trae:` prefix. |
| Are `/trae:` prefixes ONLY a Claude Code concept? | **YES.** `/trae:name` is Claude Code's **slash command** syntax. It is the name users type in Claude Code's chat UI (e.g., `/trae:run "task"`). Under the hood, Claude Code executes `node dist/index.js run`. |
| Does anything in OpenCode ever invoke via `/trae:` ? | **Absolutely nothing.** The only references to `/trae:` in OpenCode code are two description strings meant to tell the agent "a user might use `/trae:status` in Claude Code to find a task ID." |

**Key architectural insight:**
- **Claude Code**: User types `/trae:run "task"` (slash command) -> Claude Code executes `node dist/index.js run "task"`
- **OpenCode**: Agent uses `trae-run` tool or follows `commands/trae-run.md` instructions -> Tool directly executes `node dist/index.js run "task"`
- **Shared**: Both platforms ultimately call `node dist/index.js <raw-command>` -- the `/trae:` prefix exists ONLY as Claude Code's slash command syntax.
</task_result>
state.title: Verify OpenCode command format

parts.text:
The analysis is very clear:

- **OpenCode does NOT use `/trae:run` format.** It does NOT support `/trae:` prefix at all.
- OpenCode uses `trae-run`, `trae-review`, etc. as **Bun tools** (via `.opencode/tools/*.ts`), or uses the command definitions in `.opencode/commands/trae-run.md` which show `node dist/index.js run $ARGUMENTS`.
- The `/trae:` prefix is **ONLY** a Claude Code slash command concept.

So I need to correct my earlier statement in the README where I said "Both Claude Code and OpenCode support the same `/trae:*` command interface".

源码级验证结论：

**OpenCode 不支持 `/trae:run` 形式。** `/trae:` 前缀仅是 Claude Code 的 **slash command** 语法，OpenCode 从未使用。

| 平台 | 用户触发方式 | OpenCode Agent 调用方式 | 最终 CLI |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Claude Code** | 用户输入 `/trae:run "任务"` | AI 通过 MCP 工具 `trae_run` | `node dist/index.js run "任务"` |
| **OpenCode** | 无 `/trae:` 概念 | Agent 调用 `trae-run` 工具 或读取 `.opencode/commands/trae-run.md` | `node dist/index.js run "任务"` |

**关键源码证据：**

1. `.opencode/tools/trae-run.ts:44` → `["node", DIST_INDEX, "run"]` （无前缀）
2. `.opencode/commands/trae-run.md` → `node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" run $ARGUMENTS` （无前缀）
3. `src/index.ts` CLI 路由 → `trae-plugin-cc <command>` 要求原始命令名（`run`、`sessions` 等），不接受 `/trae:` 格式

之前 README 中 "Both Claude Code and OpenCode support the same `/trae:*` command interface" 的说法是**不准确的**，需要修正。需要我修改吗？

diffs.patch:
Index: README.md =================================================================== --- README.md +++ README.md @@ -1,348 +1,348 @@ # trae-plugin-cc The plugin bridges [**Claude Code**](https://claude.ai/code) and [**OpenCode**](https://opencode.ai) with ByteDance [**Trae Agent**](https://docs.trae.cn/cli) (`trae-cli`). Delegate natural language tasks, run code reviews, manage sessions, and enable Agent-to-Agent communication — all inheriting trae-cli's OAuth2 auth (no extra API keys needed). ## Features | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Task Delegation** | Send natural language prompts to Trae Agent with YOLO mode, session resume, git worktree isolation, and fine-grained tool permissions | | **Code Review** | Automatic git diff + professional review — standard or adversarial (extremely strict), with smart base branch detection and change-size estimation | | **Session Management** | Query trae-cli's history: conversations, tool calls, context summaries, topic search, and bulk cleanup | | **ACP Protocol** | JSON-RPC over STDIO for inter-agent communication (start/stop servers, discover agents, execute/stream tasks) | | **Background Tasks** | Long-running tasks with PID tracking, status listing, result retrieval, and cancellation | | **Lifecycle Hooks** | Auto-triggered on session start/end/stop: env checks, background task reminders, uncommitted-change gates | ## Architecture ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ trae-plugin-cc │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Claude Code │ │ OpenCode │ │ │ ├──────────────────────┤ ├──────────────────────────┤ │ -│ │ 10 Slash Commands │ │ 10 Commands (/trae:*) │ │ -│ │ 4 MCP Tools (.json) │ │ 9 Bun Tools (.ts) │ │ -│ │ 4 Lifecycle Hooks │ │ Event Hooks (.ts) │ │ +│ │ 10 Slash Commands │ │ 9 Tools + 10 Commands │ │ +│ │ commands/*.md │ │ tools/ + commands/ │ │ +│ │ 4 MCP Tools (.json) │ │ Event Hooks (.ts) │ │ +│ │ 4 Lifecycle Hooks │ │ plugins/ │ │ │ └──────────┬───────────┘ └──────────┬───────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌──────────▼──────────────────────────────────▼───────────────┐ │ │ │ Core CLI (src/ → dist/index.js) │ │ │ │ setup | run | review | adversarial-review | sessions | │ │ │ │ acp | status | result | cancel | rescue | hooks │ │ │ └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────▼──────────┐ │ │ │ trae-cli │ │ │ │ (OAuth2 inherit) │ │ │ └────────────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` -Both platforms share the same core CLI (`dist/index.js`) with unified `/trae:*` command interface. Claude Code additionally supports MCP tools for AI-autonomous invocation. +Both platforms share the same core CLI (`dist/index.js`). Claude Code uses slash commands (`/trae:*`), MCP tools for AI-autonomous invocation, and lifecycle hooks; OpenCode uses Bun tools (`.opencode/tools/`) and command descriptions (`.opencode/commands/`) — all ultimately invoking `node dist/index.js `. ## Prerequisites - **Node.js 18+** (runtime) - **trae-cli** installed and authenticated ```bash # Verify trae-cli trae-cli --help # Verify auth (should show model + login URL) cat ~/.trae/trae_cli.yaml ``` ## Installation ### Claude Code **Option 1: Marketplace (recommended)** ```bash claude plugin marketplace add https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc claude plugin install trae claude plugin list | grep trae ``` Scope: `--scope user` (global) | `--scope project` (current project) | `--scope local` (current session) **Option 2: Local directory** ```bash git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc.git cd trae-plugin-cc && npm install && npm run build claude plugin marketplace add "$(pwd)" claude plugin install trae ``` ### OpenCode ```bash git clone https://github.com/wangxumarshall/trae-plugin-cc.git cd trae-plugin-cc && npm install && npm run build cd .opencode && npm install && cd .. ``` OpenCode auto-discovers tools in `.opencode/tools/` and commands in `.opencode/commands/`. ### Verify Installation ```bash node dist/index.js setup ``` Expected output: ``` ✅ trae-cli 已安装并可用！ ## 认证状态 已认证: ✅ 模型: GLM-5 登录地址: https://console.enterprise.trae.cn ``` ## Quick Start -Both Claude Code and OpenCode support the same `/trae:*` command interface: +**Claude Code** (slash commands — user types directly): ``` /trae:run "重构用户认证模块" # execute task /trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo # skip tool confirmations /trae:review # standard review /trae:adversarial-review # adversarial review /trae:sessions list # list sessions /trae:acp run "分析代码质量" # execute via ACP ``` -> **Note**: Claude Code additionally registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation beyond what the user can trigger manually. +Claude Code additionally auto-registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation. +**OpenCode** (Bun tools — agent calls tools): + +| Tool | CLI | Description | +|------|-----|-------------| +| `trae-setup` | `setup` | Check env & auth | +| `trae-run` | `run` | Execute task | +| `trae-review` | `review` / `adversarial-review` | Code review | +| `trae-sessions` | `sessions` | Session management (9 actions) | +| `trae-acp` | `acp` | ACP protocol (6 actions) | +| `trae-status` | `status` | Background task status | +| `trae-result` | `result` | Get task output | +| `trae-cancel` | `cancel` | Cancel task | +| `trae-rescue` | `rescue` | Failure diagnosis | + +OpenCode's TUI `/` panel lists available tools from `.opencode/tools/`; `trae-` prefix comes from filenames, not from this plugin. + ## Commands Reference -All commands are invoked as `/trae: [args]` in both Claude Code and OpenCode. +All commands map to `node dist/index.js [args]`. +In **Claude Code**, users type `/trae:` (slash commands). +In **OpenCode**, agents call `trae-` tools or follow command descriptions. ### `setup` Verify trae-cli installation and auth status. -```bash -/trae:setup -``` +
Claude Code/trae:setup
OpenCodetrae-setup tool (no args)
--- ### `run "prompt"` Delegate a natural language task to Trae Agent. -```bash -/trae:run "描述任务" [options] -``` +
Claude Code/trae:run "描述任务" [options]
OpenCodetrae-run tool with prompt + options
| Option | Alias | Description | |--------|-------|-------------| | `--background` | | Run in background (returns job ID) | | `--json` | | Structured JSON output with `session_id` | | `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool permission confirmations | | `--resume [ID]` | | Resume session (omit ID = auto-resume latest) | | `--session-id ` | | Specify new session ID | | `--worktree [name]` | `-w` | Isolated git worktree (omit name = `__auto__`) | | `--allowed-tool ` | | Auto-approve tool (repeatable) | | `--disallowed-tool ` | | Auto-reject tool (repeatable) | | `--query-timeout ` | | Per-query timeout (e.g. `30s`, `5m`) | | `--bash-tool-timeout ` | | Bash tool timeout | | `-c ` | | Config override (repeatable) | | `--inject-context ` | | Inject another session's context | **Examples:** ```bash /trae:run "重构认证模块" --background --json /trae:run "继续" --resume --yolo /trae:run "修复 bug" --worktree --allowed-tool Edit --allowed-tool Bash ``` --- ### `review` / `adversarial-review` Code review with automatic git diff. -```bash -/trae:review [options] # standard -/trae:adversarial-review [options] # adversarial (strict) -``` +
Claude Code/trae:review or /trae:adversarial-review
OpenCodetrae-review tool with `adversarial: true/false`
| Option | Alias | Description | |--------|-------|-------------| | `--base ` | | Base branch (auto-detected: main/master/develop) | | `--background` | | Run in background (recommended for large diffs) | | `--yolo` | `-y` | Skip tool confirmations | | `--json` | | Structured JSON output | | `--session-id ` | | Specify session ID | Auto-detects base branch and estimates review size, recommending background mode for large changes. --- ### `sessions ` Manage trae-cli's historical sessions (reads from file cache directly — no HTTP calls). -```bash -/trae:sessions [options] -``` +
Claude Code/trae:sessions [options]
OpenCodetrae-sessions tool with `action` + args
| Action | Args | Options | Description | |--------|------|---------|-------------| | `list` | | `--cwd`, `--limit` (default 20) | List all sessions | | `recent` | | `--cwd` | Most recent session for cwd | | `detail ` | `` | | Session metadata + event counts | | `conversation ` | `` | `--limit` (default 50) | Conversation messages | | `tools ` | `` | | Tool call stats + records (up to 30) | | `context ` | `` | | Full context summary | | `find ` | `` | | Search by keyword | | `delete ` | `` | | Delete a session | | `delete-smoke` | | | Bulk delete sessions with "smoke" in ID/title | --- ### `acp ` ACP protocol: **JSON-RPC over STDIO** (not HTTP). Enables Agent-to-Agent communication. -```bash -/trae:acp [options] -``` +
Claude Code/trae:acp [options]
OpenCodetrae-acp tool with `action` + args
| Action | Args | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `start` | `--yolo`, `--allowed-tool`, `--disabled-tool` | Start ACP Server (blocks, keeps STDIO pipe open) | | `stop` | | Stop ACP Server | | `status` | | Check server status | | `agents` | | Discover available agents (auto-starts server if needed) | | `run ` | `` | Execute task via ACP (auto-starts server) | | `stream ` | `` | Stream task output in real-time (auto-starts server) | > **Note**: `acp start` is a blocking call — it holds the process alive for the STDIO pipe. Use `agents`, `run`, or `stream` for interactive use (they auto-start the server). --- ### `status` List all background tasks (reads `.claude-trae-plugin/` for PID files). -```bash -/trae:status -``` +
Claude Code/trae:status
OpenCodetrae-status tool
--- ### `result ` Get output of a background task. -```bash -/trae:result -``` +
Claude Code/trae:result
OpenCodetrae-result tool with `task_id`
--- ### `cancel ` Force-kill a background task (SIGKILL). -```bash -/trae:cancel -``` +
Claude Code/trae:cancel
OpenCodetrae-cancel tool with `task_id`
--- ### `rescue` Diagnose recent task failures, collect error logs and git status. -```bash -/trae:rescue [--context text] -``` +
Claude Code/trae:rescue [--context text]
OpenCodetrae-rescue tool with optional `context`
| Option | Description | |--------|-------------| | `--context ` | Additional context for diagnosis | --- ### Lifecycle Hooks Auto-triggered — no manual invocation needed. | Hook | Trigger | Behavior | |------|---------|----------| | `SessionStart` | Claude Code startup / OpenCode session.created | Check trae-cli, remind background tasks | | `SessionEnd` | Claude Code exit / OpenCode session.deleted | Clean old logs, remind running tasks | | `Stop` | User stops operation | Gate on uncommitted changes | | `PostToolUse` | After `/trae:review` | Log review results | --- ### `hooks ` (internal) Internal hook handler — invoked by lifecycle scripts, not users. ```bash trae-plugin-cc hooks session-start|session-end|stop-gate ``` ## Project Structure ``` trae-plugin-cc/ ├── .claude-plugin/ # Claude Code plugin + marketplace manifest ├── commands/ # Slash command docs (*.md) ├── .mcp.json # 4 MCP tool definitions ├── hooks/hooks.json # Claude Code lifecycle hooks ├── scripts/ # Hook scripts (*.mjs) ├── src/ # Core CLI (TypeScript → dist/index.js) │ ├── index.ts # Entrypoint │ ├── commands/ # Command handlers │ └── utils/ # TraeExecutor, SessionReader, AuthBridge, AcpClient... ├── .opencode/ # OpenCode integration │ ├── tools/ # 9 Bun tools (*.ts) │ ├── commands/ # 10 command docs (*.md) │ ├── plugins/ # Event hooks │ └── package.json # OpenCode deps (@opencode-ai/plugin) └── tests/ # Jest tests ``` ## Development ```bash npm run build # esbuild → dist/index.js (run after ANY src/ change) npm test # jest (all tests) cd .opencode && npm install && cd .. # OpenCode deps (once) ``` ### ⚠️ Build Rule `npm run build` uses **esbuild** (bundled single file with shebang) — this is the **production entrypoint**. `npm run build:tsc` produces separate `.js` files for module imports but is not used at runtime. | Command | Output | Used By | |---------|--------|---------| | `npm run build` | `dist/index.js` (bundled) | **Runtime CLI** | | `npm run build:tsc` | `dist/**/*.js` (separate) | Direct module imports | **Always run `npm run build` after modifying `src/` before testing.** This applies to OpenCode too — its tools spawn `dist/index.js`. ## Session Cache Paths trae-cli stores sessions on disk (read directly by this plugin): | Platform | Path | |----------|------| | macOS | `~/Library/Caches/trae_cli/sessions/` | | Linux | `~/.cache/trae_cli/sessions/` | ## parts.text: 为什么我在opencode中通过 slash 能看到所有trae-xx的命令
OpenCode 也支持 `/trae:xx` 这种用法，我需要检查 OpenCode 的代码来理解它为什么会这样工作。

input.prompt:
Search ALL code in the project thoroughly (src/, .opencode/, commands/, *.json, *.md, etc.) for the following:

1. Find where OpenCode registers `/trae:` commands or slash commands. Is there a plugin.json, package.json, opencode.json, or any command registry that maps `/trae:xxx` to the actual command implementation?

2. In `.opencode/commands/`, each .md file has frontmatter `description:`. This is how OpenCode registers command descriptions for the agent. Does OpenCode automatically convert these into slash commands prefixed with `/trae:`? Look for any pattern or convention.

3. Read ALL 10 `.opencode/commands/*.md` files fully. Does ANY of them contain `/trae:` prefix in the content? Or is the user interface somehow adding `/trae:` as a convention?

4. Check if the `.opencode/tools/*.ts` files define any command metadata that could register `/` commands.

5. Look for any configuration that prefixes commands with `/trae:`.

6. Check the AGENTS.md file for OpenCode integration documentation about how commands become slash commands.

Return the exact mechanism by which OpenCode exposes `/trae:xxx` commands. If you can't find it in the codebase, explain that this is handled by OpenCode's framework itself (outside this repo), and describe what this repo provides to enable it.

state.output:
task_id: REDACTED (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Now I have the complete picture. Let me provide a thorough answer.

---

## Findings: How `/trae:xxx` Commands Are Registered

### 1. No `/trae:` Registration Exists in This Codebase for OpenCode

After searching all 179+ matches across the entire repository, **there is zero code that programmatically registers `/trae:` as a slash command prefix for OpenCode**. Specifically:

- No `plugin.json`, `opencode.json`, or any command registry file exists.
- The `@opencode-ai/plugin` package's `tool()` function (defined in `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/tool.d.ts`) takes `{ description, args, execute }` with **no `name` field**.
- The `Hooks` interface (in `index.d.ts`) has `tool`, `event`, `config`, `auth`, `provider`, and various lifecycle hooks -- but **no `command` or `slashCommand` registration mechanism**.

### 2. The Two Platforms Use Completely Different Mechanisms

**Claude Code** (where `/trae:` is real slash commands):

The `commands/*.md` files define slash commands. For example, `commands/run.md` (line 6):

```markdown
# /trae:run
```

Claude Code's plugin system reads `commands/*.md`, uses the `# /trae:run` heading as the slash command name, and registers it. This is documented in `AGENTS.md` line 165:

> Defined in `commands/*.md`, user-triggered with `/trae:*`

**OpenCode** (where `/trae:` is a user-facing convention, not a registration):

The `.opencode/commands/*.md` files contain **no `/trae:` prefix anywhere**. Read all 10 files:

| File | Title/Heading | Frontmatter `description:` |
|------|--------------|---------------------------|
| `trae-run.md` | None | "Delegates a natural-language task to Trae Agent..." |
| `trae-review.md` | None | "Performs a standard professional code review..." |
| `trae-adversarial-review.md` | None | "Performs an adversarial code review..." |
| `trae-sessions.md` | None | "Lists or queries Trae Agent session history..." |
| `trae-acp.md` | None | "Manages ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) server..." |
| `trae-setup.md` | None | "Checks trae-cli installation and authentication state..." |
| `trae-status.md` | None | "Views status of background Trae tasks..." |
| `trae-result.md` | None | "Retrieves output logs for a specific background Trae task..." |
| `trae-cancel.md` | None | "Force-cancels a running background Trae task..." |
| `trae-rescue.md` | None | "Diagnoses failed Trae tasks and suggests recovery..." |

None of them have `# /trae:xxx` headings. They only contain instructions like:

```bash
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" run $ARGUMENTS
```

### 3. How OpenCode Actually Discovers and Exposes Commands

OpenCode uses a **file-convention-based discovery system** -- not a registration-based system:

| Directory | What OpenCode Does |
|-----------|--------------------|
| `.opencode/tools/*.ts` | Bun-run TS files that `export default tool({...})`. Each file becomes an **invocable tool** whose name is derived from the **filename** (e.g., `trae-run.ts` -> tool name `trae-run`). The `@opencode-ai/plugin` `tool()` function is a **pure passthrough** (see `tool.js`: `return input`) -- it adds no registration logic. |
| `.opencode/commands/*.md` | Markdown files with `description:` frontmatter. These provide **agent-readable descriptions** of what commands exist and how to invoke the CLI. OpenCode reads them and makes them available to the AI agent's context. |
| `.opencode/plugins/*.ts` | Event hook plugins (export `Plugin`). These handle lifecycle events like `session.created`, `session.deleted`. |

The `@opencode-ai/plugin` package is **not a registration framework** -- it is a thin type-safe wrapper. The `tool()` function literally just returns its input. OpenCode's host application (outside this repo) scans these directories and constructs the tool/command registry itself.

### 4. Where the `/trae:` Prefix in OpenCode Comes From

The `/trae:` prefix in the OpenCode context is a **user-interface convention** handled by OpenCode's framework (outside this repo), not by this plugin. Here is the exact mechanism:

1. **This repo provides**: 
   - **9 tools** in `.opencode/tools/*.ts` (e.g., `trae-run.ts` exporting `tool({ description: "...", args: {...}, execute: ... })`)
   - **10 command descriptions** in `.opencode/commands/*.md` (each with `description:` frontmatter and shell execution instructions)
   - **1 event hook plugin** in `.opencode/plugins/trae-hooks.ts` (exporting `TraeHooksPlugin: Plugin`)

2. **OpenCode's framework (external)**:
   - Scans `.opencode/tools/*.ts` for `export default tool(...)` and registers each as an invokable tool
   - Reads `.opencode/commands/*.md` frontmatter to understand available commands
   - Displays them in the TUI with the `/` command interface, likely prefixing with `/trae:` based on the filename pattern or plugin name

3. **The CLI arguments**: The tools call `dist/index.js run`, `dist/index.js review`, etc. -- without any `/trae:` prefix. The internal CLI (`src/index.ts`) accepts clean subcommand names: `run`, `review`, `adversarial-review`, `sessions`, `acp`, `status`, `result`, `cancel`, `rescue`, `hooks`.

### 5. Summary: Exact Mechanism

**This repo does NOT register `/trae:` commands for OpenCode.** The `/trae:` prefix is applied by OpenCode's host application when it presents these tools/commands in its user interface. This repo provides:

| What | Where | How OpenCode Uses It |
|------|-------|---------------------|
| **Tool definitions** | `.opencode/tools/trae-{run,review,sessions,acp,setup,status,result,cancel,rescue}.ts` | `export default tool({...})` -- OpenCode discovers via file scanning |
| **Command descriptions** | `.opencode/commands/trae-{run,review,adversarial-review,sessions,acp,setup,status,result,cancel,rescue}.md` | Frontmatter `description:` + shell execution instructions for agent context |
| **Event hooks** | `.opencode/plugins/trae-hooks.ts` | `export const TraeHooksPlugin: Plugin` -- mapped to OpenCode lifecycle events |
| **Core CLI** | `src/` -> `dist/index.js` | Spawned by tools via `node dist/index.js <command> [args]` |

The `/trae:` prefix is a **naming convention in documentation and user-facing text** (like error messages: "Use `/trae:status` to check status") and an **OpenCode UI convention** for presenting plugin commands. It is **not** programmatically registered anywhere in this codebase.
</task_result>
state.title: Find OpenCode slash command registration

parts.text: OpenCode framework automatically exposes commands as slash commands based on file conventions; I need to update the README to accurately explain this.

答案清楚了：**`/trae:` 前缀是 Claude Code 原生的 slash command 机制，而 OpenCode 的 `/` 命令界面是其 TUI 框架自身对外展示的统一入口**，与本项目无关。

本项目对两个平台的贡献不同：

| 平台 | 命令怎么来 |
|---|---|
| **Claude Code** | `commands/*.md` 文件，如 `run.md` 含 `# /trae:run`，Claude Code 原生解析 `commands/` 目录生成 slash commands |
| **OpenCode** | `.opencode/commands/*.md` 提供 `description:` frontmatter 给 Agent 参考，`.opencode/tools/*.ts` 提供可调用工具。OpenCode TUI 的 `/` 面板只是把所有可用工具集中列出，`trae-` 前缀来自文件名 |

也就是说：**OpenCode 里没有真正的 `/trae:run` 路由注册**，你在 slash 面板看到 `trae-run`/`trae-review` 等是 OpenCode 框架从文件名自动派生的工具名，不是 slash command。

---

需要修正 README 之前 "OpenCode 也支持 `/trae:*` command interface" 的不准确描述。我直接改：

## Quick Start

Both Claude Code and OpenCode support the same `/trae:*` command interface:

```
/trae:run "重构用户认证模块"           # execute task
/trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo         # skip tool confirmations
/trae:review                           # standard review
/trae:adversarial-review               # adversarial review
/trae:sessions list                    # list sessions
/trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
```

> **Note**: Claude Code additionally registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation beyond what the user can trigger manually.

state.title: README.md

parts.text: Quick Start section still has inaccurate claim about '/trae:*' in OpenCode, needs correction with accurate platform-specific examples.

input.newString:
## Quick Start

**Claude Code** (slash commands — user types directly):

```
/trae:run "重构用户认证模块"           # execute task
/trae:run "修复登录bug" --yolo         # skip tool confirmations
/trae:review                           # standard review
/trae:adversarial-review               # adversarial review
/trae:sessions list                    # list sessions
/trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
```

Claude Code additionally auto-registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation.

**OpenCode** (Bun tools — agent calls tools):

| Tool | CLI | Description |
|------|-----|-------------|
| `trae-setup` | `setup` | Check env & auth |
| `trae-run` | `run` | Execute task |
| `trae-review` | `review` / `adversarial-review` | Code review |
| `trae-sessions` | `sessions` | Session management (9 actions) |
| `trae-acp` | `acp` | ACP protocol (6 actions) |
| `trae-status` | `status` | Background task status |
| `trae-result` | `result` | Get task output |
| `trae-cancel` | `cancel` | Cancel task |
| `trae-rescue` | `rescue` | Failure diagnosis |

OpenCode's TUI `/` panel lists available tools from `.opencode/tools/`; `trae-` prefix comes from filenames, not from this plugin.

/trae:acp run "分析代码质量"            # execute via ACP
```

> **Note**: Claude Code additionally registers 4 MCP tools (`trae_run`, `trae_review`, `trae_sessions`, `trae_acp`) for AI-autonomous invocation beyond what the user can trigger manually.

parts.text:
Now let me count both platforms side by side.

**Claude Code:**
- Slash commands: 10 (commands/*.md: acp, adversarial-review, cancel, rescue, result, review, run, sessions, setup, status)
- MCP tools: 4 (.mcp.json: trae_run, trae_review, trae_sessions, trae_acp)
- Lifecycle hooks: 4 (hooks/hooks.json: SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, PostToolUse)

**OpenCode:**
- Bun tools: 9 (.opencode/tools/*: trae-acp, tra-cancel, tra-rescue, tra-result, tra-review, tra-run, tra-sessions, tra-setup, tra-status)


- Command descriptions: 10 (.opencode/commands/*.md)
- Event hooks: 1 (.opencode/plugins/trae-hooks.ts)

The differences come down to how each platform handles specific functionality. Claude Code has adversarial-review as a separate slash command and MCP tool, whereas OpenCode's trae-review tool just accepts an adversarial flag to toggle that behavior. The command descriptions are a documentation layer in OpenCode that Claude Code doesn't have, and the plugin file is OpenCode's equivalent to Claude's individual lifecycle hooks.

原因很简单：**两个平台的集成机制不同，同一功能在两边有不同的实现方式。**

逐个比对：

| 类型 | Claude Code | OpenCode | 差异原因 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **命令** | 10 个 | 10 个（命令文档） | 一致 |
| **可调用工具** | 4 个 MCP + 10 个 Slash | 9 个 Bun 工具 | MCP 是 AI 自主调用的接口，Slash 是用户手动触发的，两者不是一回事；而 OpenCode 的 Bun 工具融合了这两者的功能 |
| **生命周期钩子** | 4 个（hooks.json） | 1 个插件（trae-hooks.ts） | Claude Code 把钩子拆成 4 个独立注册；OpenCode 用单个插件对象注册所有事件监听 |

关键差异点：

**`adversarial-review` 是 Claude Code 独有的独立 Slash Command**（`commands/adversarial-review.md`），但 OpenCode 没有对应的独立工具文件。因为 OpenCode 的 `trae-review` 工具内部有一个 `adversarial` 布尔参数来切换模式——**一个工具做了两个命令的事**。

**MCP 工具只有 4 个**（trae_run, trae_review, trae_sessions, trae_acp），没有 status/result/cancel/rescue/setup，因为这些是辅助操作，不需要 AI 自主调用，用户手动触发 `/trae:status` 等 slash command 就够
为什么opencode上这样实现：OpenCode 的 trae-review 工具内部有一个 adversarial 布尔参数来切换模式——一个工具做了两个命令的事。

I need to answer why OpenCode implements `trae-review` with an `adversarial` boolean parameter instead of having two separate tools/commands like Claude Code does. Let me check the actual OpenCode tool implementation and compare it to Claude Code's approach.

<task_result>
Now I have the complete picture. Here is the analysis:

---

## Analysis: Why `trae-review` uses an `adversarial` boolean instead of separate commands

### 1. Code Flow in `.opencode/tools/trae-review.ts`

The routing happens on a single line (line 25):

```typescript
const command = args.adversarial ? "adversarial-review" : "review"
const cliArgs: string[] = ["node", DIST_INDEX, command]
```

The complete flow:
1. OpenCode presents the agent with **one tool** named `trae-review` (filename-derived)
2. The tool schema defines 6 args: `base_branch`, `adversarial` (boolean), `background`, `yolo`, `json_output`, `session_id`
3. When the agent calls the tool, `args.adversarial` determines which CLI command gets invoked:
   - `adversarial: false` (or omitted) -> `node dist/index.js review`
   - `adversarial: true` -> `node dist/index.js adversarial-review`
4. Remaining flags are passed through identically to either command.

### 2. Command Description Files: Yes, There Are TWO Separate Files

| File | Invokes |
|------|---------|
| `.opencode/commands/trae-review.md` | `node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" review $ARGUMENTS` |
| `.opencode/commands/trae-adversarial-review.md` | `node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/index.js" adversarial-review $ARGUMENTS` |

Both exist and are properly formatted. However, the tool `trae-review.ts` does **not** reference these command description files at all. The `commands/` directory is agent-readable documentation; the `tools/` directory is what the agent actually invokes. They operate independently.

### 3. This Pattern Exists Across BOTH Platforms

**Claude Code's MCP tool (`.mcp.json`)** also uses the same merged approach: only ONE MCP tool called `trae_review` with an `adversarial: boolean` parameter:

```json
{
  "name": "trae_review",
  "parameters": {
    "properties": {
      "adversarial": { "type": "boolean", "description": "对抗性审查模式，极度严苛地检查代码" }
    }
  }
}
```

**Slash commands** are the exception -- Claude Code has `/trae:review` AND `/trae:adversarial-review` as two separate slash commands (user-facing). But for agent-invocable tools (MCP on Claude Code, Bun tools on OpenCode), both use the merged boolean pattern.

### 4. Mapping Summary from README.md (line 129)

The README at line 129 explicitly documents this:

```
| `trae-review` | `review` / `adversarial-review` | Code review |
```

And at line 187:

```
Claude Code: /trae:review or /trae:adversarial-review
OpenCode:    trae-review tool with `adversarial: true/false`
```

### 5. Was This Intentional? YES -- The Evidence

**Git history proves this was the original design from the initial OpenCode port.** The initial commit `43a5609` commit message explicitly states:

```
Phase 1 - OpenCode Commands (10 files):
- .opencode/commands/trae-{run,review,adversarial-review,sessions,acp,setup,status,result,cancel,rescue}.md

Phase 2 - OpenCode Tools (4 files):
- .opencode/tools/trae-review.ts: trae_review + trae_adversarial_review
```

The phrase "trae_review + trae_adversarial_review" confirms the intent was to combine both review modes into one tool from day one. The file has been unchanged in structure since then.

### 6. Why This Design? Likely Reasons

The codebase exhibits the same merging pattern elsewhere. Note these parallels:

| Tool | CLI Commands it Maps To | Merged Via |
|------|------------------------|------------|
| `trae-review` | `review` + `adversarial-review` | `adversarial: boolean` |
| `trae-sessions` | 9 different actions (`list`, `detail`, `conversation`, `tools`, `context`, `recent`, `find`, `delete`, `delete-smoke`) | `action: enum` |
| `trae-acp` | 6 different actions (`start`, `stop`, `status`, `agents`, `run`, `stream`) | `action: enum` |

**The consistent pattern**: For multi-action concepts (sessions, ACP) or two-mode concepts (review types), the plugin uses **ONE tool with a routing parameter** (enum or boolean) rather than a tool per CLI command.

Likely design reasoning:
- **Fewer tools = less token overhead.** Each tool definition consumes context window tokens. 9 tools instead of 20+ keeps the system prompt leaner.
- **Clearer semantics for the agent.** One tool for "review" with a mode switch is more intuitive than forcing the agent to choose between two similarly-named tools.
- **Consistency with MCP approach.** The same `adversarial` boolean was already used in Claude Code's MCP tool definition, so mirroring it in OpenCode keeps parity.

### 7. The Command Files May Be Slightly Redundant

The `commands/` directory (10 files) exists for OpenCode, providing agent-readable instructions. Since the tool already encodes all the information (description + args), the command files are somewhat duplicative. They were likely created as a port of the Claude Code slash command structure ("Phase 1" in the commit message) and kept for completeness. The agent can learn everything it needs from the tool definition alone -- the command files serve as supplemental documentation.

### Conclusion

This is an **intentional design decision**, established during the initial OpenCode port. The plugin deliberately consolidates `review` + `adversarial-review` into one `trae-review` tool with a boolean `adversarial` parameter, matching the same pattern used across both platforms' agent-invocable interfaces (MCP on Claude Code, Bun tools on OpenCode). The two separate command description files exist as agent-readable documentation but are not leveraged as separate tools -- they were ported for structural symmetry with Claude Code's slash commands but the tool layer intentionally merged them.
</task_result>

### 源码事实

为什么 opencode没有mcp

state.title: Compare MCP vs OpenCode protocols

parts.text: The user asked "协议详细的差异是什么" (What are the detailed protocol differences). I already received a comprehensive analysis. I need to summarize it concisely.

**核心差异可以用一句话概括：Claude Code 是声明式的（MCP 只描述工具，执行由框架托管），OpenCode 是命令式的（工具自己写执行逻辑，框架只管调度）。**

### 工具定义层

| 维度 | MCP (`.mcp.json`) | OpenCode Bun Tool (`tool({})`) |
|---|---|---|
| 格式 | 纯 JSON | TypeScript 对象 |
| 参数语言 | JSON Schema (`type`, `properties`) | Zod (`tool.schema.string().optional()`) |
| 执行逻辑 | **无** — 由 Claude Code 将参数转 CLI 标志后调起子进程 | **有** — 自己写 `execute(args, context)`，内部 `spawn` 子进程 |
| 返回类型 | 纯文本（stdout） | `{ output: string; metadata?: Record }` 可带结构化元数据 |
| 上下文 | 无 | `ToolContext` 含 `sessionID`, `directory`, `worktree`, `abort` 等 |
| 类型安全 | 仅运行时 JSON Schema 校验 | 编译时 TypeScript 类型推断 + 运行时 Zod 校验 |

### 生命周期层

| 维度 | `hooks/hooks.json` | `.opencode/plugins/trae-hooks.ts` |
|---|---|---|
| 格式 | 声明式 JSON (`timeout`, `matcher`) | 命令式 TS (`try/catch` 事件处理器) |
| 钩子种类 | 4 种（SessionStart/End/Stop/PostToolUse） | 20+ 种（event, config, tool.*, chat.*, permission.* 等） |
| 触发方式 | 框架自动匹配 `matcher` 正则 | 代码中 `switch (event.type)` 手动路由 |
| 生命周期安全 | 框架超时自动 kill | 插件自行 `try/catch` 静默吞错 |

### 命令文档层

| 维度 | `commands/*.md` (Claude) | `.opencode/commands/trae-*.md` (OpenCode) |
|---|---|---|
| 标题 | `# /trae:run` | 无标题，直接正文 |
| 权限声明 | `allowed-tools: Bash(trae-cli:*)` | 无此概念 |
| 语言 | 中文 | 英文 |
| 格式 | 严格分隔 Description/Usage/Options/Examples | 自由段落 |

**总结**：MCP 是契约式协议（只描述接口，让框架去执行），Bun Tool 是函数式协议（你自己定义怎么执行）。
